opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My doctor messaged that my iron is really low and I have an autoimmune disease. My heart is racing, I can't catch my breath and I feel like I'm going to pass out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I read the results an hour ago and I'm panicking — is my heart failing? Do I need an ambulance?

symptom_narrative.onset: about an hour ago, right after reading the results
symptom_narrative.character: chest tightness, racing heart, breathing fast, tingling hands
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: tingling in fingers and around the mouth, no exertional chest pain, no leg swelling, slightly tired lately
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had panic attacks before during stress

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, tends to research symptoms online

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told her heart is fine

communication_style.typical_message_style: rapid, frightened, references what she read

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: consumer oximeter; reassuring but not a clinical assessment
